turns.content: I'm staring at the sprs crate docs and I have no idea why the CSR format is even necessary. It seems like a matrix should just be stored as-is, right? Why do I need to manually construct the indices and values?

That's a great question because if you store every single value in dense memory, you end up wasting gigabytes of space for empty spots. The sprs crate uses CSR internally which breaks the matrix down into three separate arrays: row pointers, column indices, and values. This way you only keep the actual numbers that exist instead of padding zeros.
Okay so I just dump all my data into a Vec? But what about operations like matrix multiplication? Does it even support that?

You can definitely do arithmetic with sprs matrices, but the implementation details are tricky. When you multiply two sparse matrices using CSR format, the algorithm iterates through row pointers and column indices to find non-zero intersections without touching zero entries. It's not a simple loop though; it requires careful tracking of which columns have been processed.
Right so I'm building a graph adjacency list for some kind of network analysis and I want to map that directly to the matrix. Can I just use sprs for that?

Absolutely, you can treat your graph edges as non-zero entries in a sparse matrix where rows represent source nodes and columns represent destination nodes. The CSR structure makes this perfect because the column indices will naturally correspond to node IDs while the values can store edge weights or connection types.
So if I have 10 million edges but only a few thousand unique connections, does that mean CSR actually saves me something?

Yes, in your scenario the dense representation would consume massive memory just storing those zeros. With sprs and CSR you effectively skip over all the empty space by only indexing the actual connections. The trade-off is that operations become more complex algorithmically but the storage efficiency is huge.
Got it, so I should probably check if sprs has a convenience function or do I need to handle all this myself?

sprs provides helper methods for converting between sparse formats and performing basic arithmetic, but you still need to implement the logic for your specific graph traversal patterns. The crate gives you the building blocks like Serialize traits and custom iterators so you can build specialized operations on top of the raw CSR arrays.
